SavingsFor continuation of benefit payments notwithstanding repeals made by , see , set out as a note under .section 8512 of Pub. L. 116–283section 8512(b)(1) of Pub. L. 116–283section 725 of this title

Increase in Widows Benefits and Effective Date of Increase, , , provided:Pub. L. 94–17089 Stat. 1022Dec. 23, 1975“That the benefits payable under section 1 or (, 466), as amended (, 772) shall, effective on the first day of the calendar month following enactment of this Act [], be increased by $26 per month.section 2 of the Act of August 19, 195064 Stat. 46533 U.S.C. 771Dec. 23, 1975“Sec. 2.The increases under this Act shall apply to benefits which commence before, on, or after the date of enactment of this Act [], but no increase in benefits shall be paid for any period prior to the date of enactment of this Act [], or the date on which the eligibility for benefits commences, whichever is later.”Dec. 23, 1975Dec. 23, 1975

Cost-of-Living Adjustment to Widow’s Annuity, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–163, § 281 Stat. 518Nov. 29, 1967“Each annuity payable under the Act entitled ‘An Act to provide benefits for widows of certain persons who were retired or are eligible for retirement under section 6 of the Act entitled “An Act to authorize aids to navigation and for other works in the Lighthouse Service, and for other purposes”, approved , as amended’, approved (), and each annuity payable under section 6 of the Act entitled ‘An Act to authorize aids to navigation and for other works in the Lighthouse Service, and for other purposes’, approved (), shall be increased by the same percentage, adjusted to the nearest dollar, and on the same effective date, as each increase hereafter allowed under the cost-of-living annuity adjustment provisions of , United States Code.”June 20, 1918August 19, 1950June 20, 191833 U.S.C. 771–77533 U.S.C. 763section 8340(b) of title 5

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

Transfer of FunctionsBureau of Lighthouses, of which Lighthouse Service was a part, transferred and consolidated with Coast Guard under Secretary of the Treasury by Reorg. Plan No. II of 1939, § 2(a), eff. , 4 F.R. 2731, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
